title: Rename misleading variable 'data' to 'user_records'
description: The variable 'data' in the user sync module was ambiguous. Renamed to 'user_records' to clearly indicate it holds a list of user record dictionaries.
diff:
diff --git a/sync/user_sync.py b/sync/user_sync.py
index 7f8a9b0..c1d2e3f 100644
--- a/sync/user_sync.py
+++ b/sync/user_sync.py
@@ -18,12 +18,12 @@ def sync_users(source_url: str, batch_size: int = 100) -> int:
     '''Sync user records from remote source in batches.'''
     total_synced = 0
 
-    data = fetch_remote_users(source_url)
-    if not data:
+    user_records = fetch_remote_users(source_url)
+    if not user_records:
         logger.warning("No users fetched from %s", source_url)
         return 0
 
-    for batch in chunked(data, batch_size):
+    for batch in chunked(user_records, batch_size):
         inserted = upsert_batch(batch)
         total_synced += inserted
         logger.info("Synced batch of %d users", inserted)
author: peter
